title: InteractionKit: A Methodological Infrastructure for HAI Trust Calibration
oneLiner: A versioned log schema and experiment platform for reproducible trust calibration experiments.
keyTakeaways: Different labs studying the same HAI interaction pattern produce incompatible behavioral data — different variable names, different logging formats, different measurement conventions.
InteractionKit provides a versioned log schema (14 columns) and an experiment platform such that two independent implementations produce data that can be merged with minimal translation.
Built on earlier work on calibration measurement (MaxFitCalib) and knowledge infrastructure (Knowledge Compiler).
Pilot and Study 1 in progress (N≥120, fitness domain, evidence-augmented vs baseline confidence display).

faq.a: Participant ID, trial ID, condition, scenario, AI advice text, confidence display, user confidence rating, user decision, response time, correctness, evidence tags, attention check, timestamp, schema version.

faq.q: How does this differ from existing experiment frameworks?
faq.a: Existing frameworks (jsPsych, lab.js, PsychoPy) focus on presentation and timing. InteractionKit focuses on data compatibility: two labs running the same study design produce data with identical column names and types.
Schema v0.3 finalized after a CHI reviewer critique and 3-agent code audit. Pilot deployment and Study 1 (N≥120, within-subject, 10 scenarios) in progress.

Design spec + PRD v0.3. Prolific URL params verified.
